qualifications.name: BEng Chemical
notes: Aggregate note: Excluding Life Orientation.

Career opportunities: Chemical engineer; Large-scale process operations; Production of chemicals, pharmaceutical products, fertilisers, fuels, metals and other materials; Effluent and waste processing and recycling.
Admission score type: Aggregated average.
Faculty aggregate note: Excluding Life Orientation.

General admission and selection: Aggregated_Average_Required: 70%; aggregate_note: Excluding Life Orientation; requirements: ["Mathematics: 70%", "Physical Sciences: 60%", "English Home Language: 50% with no Afrikaans requirement OR English First Additional Language: 60% with no Afrikaans requirement", "English First Additional Language: 50% with Afrikaans Home Language: 50% OR English First Additional Language: 50% with Afrikaans Second Additional Language: 60%"]; selection_requirements: ["Applicants are subject to selection according to selection criteria", "Selection mark is calculated using Grade 11 subjects for conditional selection or Grade 12 subjects for final selection", "Selection mark = Mathematics percentage + Physical Sciences percentage + 6 \u00d7 Matric average", "Matric average is calculated from the six best Matric subjects, excluding Life Orientation", "Maximum selection score is 800"].

Professional accreditation: body: Engineering Council of South Africa; abbreviation: ECSA; note: BEng programmes are accredited by ECSA; after relevant practical experience, graduates may register as Professional Engineers.

Extended curriculum information: {"available": true, "duration": "5 years", "applies_to": "All BEng programmes", "note": "A limited number of applicants who meet the minimum requirements for the BEng ECPs will be selected", "selection_basis": ["Selection score", "Socio-economic status", "Preference may be given to candidates from underserved educational backgrounds"]}.
Additional Stellenbosch requirements: Must meet Engineering language requirements.
